title: Roasted Crunchy Vietnamese Tofu Spring Rolls
description: A tangy and light Vietnamese dish featuring crispy tofu and fresh vegetables wrapped in rice paper, served with a sweet and spicy dipping sauce. This plant-based-friendly dish is a crowd-pleaser.

ingredients.name: firm tofu
ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small cubes

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Toss the tofu cubes with 1 tablespoon of peanut oil and roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
In a large bowl, combine the grated carrots, sliced cucumbers, chopped mint leaves, and cooked vermicelli noodles.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, and grated ginger to make the dipping sauce.
Fill a large bowl with warm water and soak the rice paper wrappers for 1 minute, or until soft and pliable.
Place a soaked rice paper wrapper on a flat surface and arrange 2-3 pieces of roasted tofu and some of the carrot and noodle mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Fold the bottom half of the wrapper up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling ingredients.
Heat the remaining peanut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and fry the spring rolls until crispy and golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Serve the spring rolls hot with the dipping sauce.
